steps.text: Clarify the concrete comparison criteria and define what "high-concurrency network service" typically entails (workload patterns, latency/throughput focus, deployment context).
Research and summarize Rust vs Go performance characteristics for high-concurrency network services (throughput, latency, scalability, runtime model, async vs goroutines, scheduling).
Research and compare Rust vs Go on memory safety (type system, ownership/borrowing vs GC, data races, typical classes of bugs, tooling like sanitizers and race detectors).
Research and compare Rust vs Go on developer ergonomics (learning curve, language complexity, tooling, ecosystem for networking, debugging/profiling, iteration speed).
Synthesize findings into a concise, opinionated comparison tailored to choosing a language for a high-concurrency network service, including trade-offs and when to prefer each.
